summary: The notebook implements a multi-stage spatial optimization pipeline for placing Christmas trees, combining an external C++ solver (bbox3) with custom Python-based local search strategies. It rigorously enforces non-overlap constraints using Shapely's spatial indexing and high-precision decimal arithmetic. The pipeline iteratively improves configurations, selects the best per-group solution across multiple submissions via a custom ensemble script, and includes a final validation step to replace any overlapping groups with a fallback submission. Readers should understand how to combine external optimizers with Python-based local search, manage strict geometric constraints, and safely ensemble spatial optimization results.
competition_flow: Loads multiple spatial placement submissions, iteratively optimizes them using an external C++ solver and custom Python local search strategies while enforcing strict non-overlap constraints, ensembles the best per-group configurations across all inputs, and validates/fallbacks any overlapping groups before saving the final submission.
data_reading: pandas.read_csv and csv.DictReader to parse id, x, y, deg columns from /kaggle/input/*/*.csv and /kaggle/input/santa-submission/submission.csv, stripping 's' prefixes and converting to floats/Decimals.
data_processing: High-precision coordinate scaling via Decimal with 1e18 factor, Shapely polygon construction/rotation/translation, strict overlap validation using STRtree, and fallback replacement of overlapping groups with a pre-validated submission.
models: bbox3 (external C++ optimizer), Simulated Annealing, Gradient Descent, Basin Hopping, Swap moves, Rotation Grid Search
loss_functions: minimize (side^2 / n) score where side is the bounding box side length per group
ensembling: Per-group (n) best-of selection across multiple input submissions, followed by a final ensemble pass over the working directory to produce the output file.
insights: Combining external C++ solvers with Python-based local search and strict geometric validation can effectively improve spatial packing scores.
High-precision decimal arithmetic prevents floating-point errors in overlap detection.
Focusing optimization efforts on the worst-performing groups yields the most significant score improvements.
critical_findings: Overlap detection is computationally expensive but mandatory; using Shapely's STRtree significantly speeds up pairwise intersection checks.
Floating-point precision issues can cause false overlap detections, necessitating Decimal with a large scale factor.
